pet.name: Kito
pet.special_needs:
Chronic respiratory disease with recent flare-ups, low body weight risk, and stress-related feather plucking. Kito needs close daily observation of breathing
  effort, appetite, droppings, energy, vocalization, and tolerance for activity. Follow the veterinarian’s existing care plan exactly. Needs a calm indoor routine,
  careful cage hygiene, humidity support as advised by the vet, reduced stress, and consistent enrichment without overexertion.
pet.characteristics: Respiratory & Nutrition: Monitor breathing effort and appetite daily, as respiratory disease increases risk of rapid decline; maintain consistent weight through frequent small meals and track droppings for signs of digestive stress. || Environment & Stress: Provide stable humidity levels per veterinary guidance and minimize loud noises or sudden activity changes, since stress directly triggers feather plucking and weakens immune response in this species. || Enrichment & Activity: Offer calm, low-exertion mental stimulation such as foraging toys or quiet training rather than high-energy play, balancing cognitive needs against fatigue and respiratory strain.
pet.species_profile_summary: African Greys typically live 40 to 60 years in captive care; this individual requires strict adherence to veterinary protocols, daily health monitoring, and a low-stress environment to manage chronic respiratory disease and prevent secondary behavioral complications.

age_context.summary: African Greys typically live 40 to 60 years in captive care; this individual requires strict adherence to veterinary protocols, daily health monitoring, and a low-stress environment to manage chronic respiratory disease and prevent secondary behavioral complications.

query:
African Grey Parrot Kito age 18 adult Chronic respiratory disease with recent flare-ups, low body weight risk, and stress-related feather plucking. Kito needs close daily observation of breathing
  effort, appetite, droppings, energy, vocalization, and tolerance for activity. Follow the veterinarian’s existing care plan exactly. Needs a calm indoor routine,
  careful cage hygiene, humidity support as advised by the vet, reduced stress, and consistent enrichment without overexertion. Respiratory & Nutrition: Monitor breathing effort and appetite daily, as respiratory disease increases risk of rapid decline; maintain consistent weight through frequent small meals and track droppings for signs of digestive stress. || Environment & Stress: Provide stable humidity levels per veterinary guidance and minimize loud noises or sudden activity changes, since stress directly triggers feather plucking and weakens immune response in this species. || Enrichment & Activity: Offer calm, low-exertion mental stimulation such as foraging toys or quiet training rather than high-energy play, balancing cognitive needs against fatigue and respiratory strain. Create a complete pet care plan with daily, weekly, monthly tasks, reminders, and safe condition-aware guidance. Owner works from home on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays and can do longer care blocks then. Tuesdays and Thursdays are busier, so check-ins should be shorter.
  Wants strong daily monitoring, one practical weekly weigh-in and cage deep-clean rhythm, monthly supply and follow-up reminders, and clear watch-for-change
  guidance for labored breathing, appetite drop, lethargy, or worsening feather damage.

goal: Create a complete pet care plan with daily, weekly, monthly tasks, reminders, and safe condition-aware guidance.
extra_context:
Owner works from home on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays and can do longer care blocks then. Tuesdays and Thursdays are busier, so check-ins should be shorter.
  Wants strong daily monitoring, one practical weekly weigh-in and cage deep-clean rhythm, monthly supply and follow-up reminders, and clear watch-for-change
  guidance for labored breathing, appetite drop, lethargy, or worsening feather damage.

 Create a complete pet care plan with daily, weekly, monthly tasks, reminders, and safe condition-aware guidance.

retrieved_passages.title: Medication Routine and Safety
retrieved_passages.content: Medication-related tasks in a home routine should focus on reminders, documentation, and observation, not dosage changes. A planning assistant can remind the owner to give medication exactly as already prescribed and to log whether the dose was completed.

retrieved_passages.title: Kidney Diet and Hydration Support
retrieved_passages.content: Owners should follow the veterinary feeding plan already prescribed and avoid changing food or supplement amounts without veterinary guidance. If a pet refuses multiple meals or shows sudden decline, the safe action is to contact a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Observation tasks are useful for special-needs pets because they create a record of appetite, water intake, energy, and bathroom habits. These tasks should be short, specific, and easy to complete consistently.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Daily care plans are stronger when they combine essential care with realistic enrichment and observation. Short play, sniff walks, brushing, or calm interaction can improve quality of life without requiring large blocks of time.

retrieved_passages.title: Puppy and Kitten Routine Basics
retrieved_passages.content: Exercise and enrichment for very young pets should stay short and positive. Puppies often do better with several brief walks or training moments instead of one long outing, and kittens benefit from short interactive play sessions that stop before exhaustion.

raw_recommendations.name: Morning health observation and appetite check
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe breathing effort, energy level, vocalization, and appetite before first meal to catch early signs of respiratory flare-up or decline.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Chronic respiratory disease requires close daily observation of breathing, appetite, and energy per special needs profile.

raw_recommendations.name: Medication administration and log
raw_recommendations.notes: Give medication exactly as prescribed by veterinarian and document completion in care log.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Medication routine requires consistent adherence to existing veterinary prescription with documentation.

raw_recommendations.name: First meal and water refresh
raw_recommendations.notes: Offer small frequent meal following veterinary feeding plan and refresh water to support weight maintenance and hydration.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Low body weight risk and respiratory disease require consistent small meals per veterinary plan and daily hydration support.

raw_recommendations.name: Droppings and appetite log check
raw_recommendations.notes: Check cage for droppings consistency and quantity; note any changes in appetite or refusal of meals.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Droppings and appetite are key indicators of digestive and respiratory health in special-needs parrots.

raw_recommendations.name: Calm enrichment and low-exertion activity
raw_recommendations.notes: Offer quiet foraging toy, gentle training, or calm interaction without high-energy play to balance cognitive needs against fatigue and respiratory strain.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Low-exertion mental stimulation supports enrichment while protecting respiratory function and reducing stress-related feather plucking.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening health check and second meal
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe breathing, energy, and tolerance for activity; offer second small meal per veterinary feeding plan.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Evening observation tracks daily trends in respiratory status and energy; frequent small meals support weight maintenance.

raw_recommendations.name: Cage hygiene and humidity check
raw_recommendations.notes: Spot-clean soiled areas, refresh perches, and verify humidity levels match veterinary guidance to reduce respiratory stress.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Clean cage and stable humidity per veterinary guidance minimize infection risk and support respiratory health in chronic disease.

raw_recommendations.name: Weekly weigh-in and body condition check
raw_recommendations.notes: Weigh Kito on same day and time each week; document weight and assess body condition to track low-weight risk trend.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Consistent weekly weigh-in on a work-from-home day allows longer care block and tracks weight maintenance critical to respiratory disease management.

raw_recommendations.name: Weekly cage deep-clean and perch refresh
raw_recommendations.notes: Full cage cleaning, perch sanitization, and substrate replacement to maintain hygiene and reduce respiratory infection risk.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Deep clean on a work-from-home day allows thorough hygiene maintenance without rushing; critical for chronic respiratory disease.

raw_recommendations.name: Monthly veterinary follow-up reminder and supply restock
raw_recommendations.notes: Check medication supply, schedule or confirm veterinary follow-up appointment, and restock food, supplements, and enrichment items per plan.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Early-month restock and follow-up scheduling ensure medication continuity and veterinary oversight of chronic respiratory disease.

raw_recommendations.name: Monthly health trend review and observation log
raw_recommendations.notes: Review daily observation logs for trends in breathing, appetite, energy,

raw_recommendations.name: Labored breathing or respiratory distress alert
raw_recommendations.notes: Watch for increased effort, wheezing, or open-mouth breathing; contact vet immediately if observed.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Chronic respiratory disease requires immediate intervention if symptoms worsen; early detection prevents rapid decline.

raw_recommendations.name: Appetite drop or meal refusal alert
raw_recommendations.notes: If Kito refuses multiple meals or shows sudden decline, contact veterinarian without delay.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Low body weight risk and respiratory disease make appetite loss a critical warning sign requiring veterinary guidance.

raw_recommendations.name: Lethargy or energy decline alert
raw_recommendations.notes: Note reduced vocalization, reluctance to move, or unusual inactivity; log and report to vet if sustained.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Energy changes indicate stress, infection, or disease progression in chronic respiratory cases and require veterinary assessment.

raw_recommendations.name: Worsening feather plucking or new bare patches alert
raw_recommendations.notes: Document location, extent, and any triggers; stress or pain may be underlying cause requiring vet evaluation.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Feather plucking is stress-related and can worsen with respiratory flare-ups; tracking changes helps vet adjust care plan.

raw_recommendations.name: Droppings abnormality alert
raw_recommendations.notes: Watch for color change, unusual consistency, or reduced output; may signal digestive stress or disease progression.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Daily droppings logging creates baseline; sudden changes warrant veterinary contact given respiratory and weight concerns.

raw_recommendations.name: Medication dose completion and observation log
raw_recommendations.notes: Document each dose given as prescribed; note any behavioral or appetite changes after administration.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Medication adherence and observation support veterinary assessment without requiring dosage changes by owner.

raw_recommendations.name: Humidity level drift or equipment failure alert
raw_recommendations.notes: If humidity drops below vet-advised range or humidifier malfunctions, adjust immediately and log change.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Stable humidity per vet guidance is critical for respiratory disease management; deviations require prompt correction.

raw_recommendations.name: Sudden noise or activity disruption alert
raw_recommendations.notes: If unexpected loud noise or household change occurs, monitor Kito for stress signs and restore calm routine.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Stress directly triggers feather plucking and weakens immune response; rapid return to calm routine minimizes impact.

raw_recommendations.name: Breathing effort and vocalization observation
raw_recommendations.notes: Listen for labored breathing, wheezing, or changes in call quality; document any strain during normal activity.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Respiratory disease requires close daily observation to catch early signs of flare-up or decline.

raw_recommendations.name: Feather condition and stress behavior check
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe for new plucking, bare patches, or excessive preening; note any correlation with activity or noise.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Stress-related feather plucking is a direct indicator of emotional or physical distress in this individual.

raw_recommendations.name: Quiet foraging or puzzle toy interaction
raw_recommendations.notes: Offer low-exertion foraging toy or puzzle feeder to provide mental stimulation without overexertion.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Calm cognitive enrichment balances mental needs against respiratory strain and fatigue.

raw_recommendations.name: Gentle perch time and calm presence
raw_recommendations.notes: Sit quietly near cage or offer gentle shoulder time if Kito initiates; avoid sudden movements or loud sounds.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Consistent low-stress interaction reduces anxiety and supports immune function without physical exertion.

raw_recommendations.name: Humidity level verification
raw_recommendations.notes: Check humidity gauge against veterinary target range; note any drift or equipment issues.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Stable humidity per vet guidance is critical for respiratory disease management.

raw_recommendations.name: Medication administration and log entry
raw_recommendations.notes: Give medication exactly as prescribed; document time and completion in daily log.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Consistent medication adherence and documentation support chronic respiratory disease management.

raw_recommendations.name: Energy level and activity tolerance assessment
raw_recommendations.notes: Note whether Kito is alert, responsive, or unusually quiet; flag any lethargy or reduced movement.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Energy changes are early indicators of respiratory decline or secondary illness in chronic disease.

raw_recommendations.name: Snack or treat offering with appetite observation
raw_recommendations.notes: Offer a small preferred snack or fresh fruit; observe interest level and consumption speed.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Frequent small meals and snacks support low body weight risk; observation tracks appetite consistency.
